items.title: Team Config Broadcast
items.description: Publish your Claude Code config to a shared team registry (git repo, S3, or local network share) so teammates can pull your setup with one command. Solves the 'how does everyone on the team get the same rules/skills/MCP servers?' problem — currently each person configures manually and drift is immediate.

items.title: New Harness Onboarding Wizard
items.description: When a user installs a new AI coding tool (Cursor, Aider, Windsurf detected via filesystem scan), HarnessSync prompts: 'We detected Cursor. Sync your Claude Code config now?' — walks through capability gaps, sets expectations, and does a one-click bootstrap. Eliminates the cold-start pain of setting up a new harness from scratch.

items.title: Sync Profiles (Work vs Personal vs OSS)
items.description: Define named profiles that determine what gets synced where. E.g., 'work' profile syncs company rules + MCP servers to all harnesses; 'personal' profile syncs hobby project skills. Activate per-repo or globally. Solves the problem of accidentally syncing work credentials or proprietary rules to personal projects.

items.title: Drift Alert Notifications
items.description: Desktop/terminal notifications when config drift is detected — e.g., 'Your Gemini GEMINI.md is 3 rules behind Claude Code. Run /sync to fix.' Currently users only discover drift reactively. Proactive alerts mean harnesses never silently fall out of sync during a long coding session.

items.title: Capability Gap Analyzer
items.description: Visual report showing exactly what Claude Code capabilities CANNOT be represented in each target harness and why. E.g., 'Codex has no equivalent for hook-based auto-triggers — 3 of your skills use this.' Helps users understand what they lose when switching harnesses, making tool selection decisions data-driven.

items.title: Cursor & Windsurf Adapter
items.description: Extend sync support to Cursor (.cursorrules, .cursor/rules/) and Windsurf (.windsurfrules) — two of the most popular AI coding IDEs. Users currently manually duplicate all their rules into these formats. With HarnessSync as the source of truth, adding Cursor/Windsurf to the sync matrix unlocks the largest user segments currently underserved.

items.title: Aider Adapter
items.description: Sync Claude Code rules and conventions to Aider's .aider.conf.yml and CONVENTIONS.md. Aider has a large open-source power-user base who also use Claude Code for complex sessions. Bridging these two means Aider sessions maintain the same project context and coding standards.

items.title: MCP Server Config Portability
items.description: Translate .mcp.json MCP server definitions into the config format of each target harness as it gains MCP support. As the ecosystem matures, every harness will adopt MCP — users who configured MCP servers in Claude Code will want them available everywhere automatically, without re-entering auth tokens and server URLs.

items.title: Config Snapshot History
items.description: Maintain a timestamped snapshot history of every sync operation — what changed, from what version, to what targets. Goes beyond the current rollback command to provide a full audit log UI. Solves 'when did this rule change and who/what triggered it?' — critical for teams using shared configs.

items.title: Smart Rule Conflict Resolver
items.description: When syncing rules that contain Claude Code-specific syntax (e.g., @tool references, skill invocations) to harnesses that don't understand them, offer AI-powered suggestions to rewrite the rule for the target. Instead of silently dropping or mangling rules, present a diff with the proposed translation and let the user approve.

items.title: CI/CD Sync GitHub Action
items.description: A GitHub Action (and pre-commit hook) that runs HarnessSync on every push to verify all target configs are up-to-date with Claude Code source. Fails the build if drift is detected. Solves the team problem where someone updates CLAUDE.md but forgets to sync, and teammates using Codex/Gemini get stale behavior.

items.title: Interactive Sync Diff Viewer
items.description: Before applying a sync, show a side-by-side diff of what will change in each target harness with syntax highlighting. User can cherry-pick which changes to apply. Currently sync is all-or-nothing — this surgical control prevents accidental overwrites of harness-specific manual customizations.

items.title: Community Config Registry
items.description: A searchable registry of shareable HarnessSync config bundles — e.g., 'Django Expert Setup', 'Rust Safety Rules', 'AWS CDK Specialist'. Users can publish their rule/skill bundles and import community packs. Lowers the barrier to getting value from AI coding tools by providing curated starting points.

items.title: Harness Performance Benchmark
items.description: Run a standardized prompt suite against all configured harnesses and report relative response quality/speed scores. Helps users understand which harness to reach for given a task type. Pairs with sync to mean 'same config everywhere, now which harness is best for this job?'

items.title: Per-File Sync Overrides
items.description: Allow rules/skills to be tagged with target annotations (e.g., `# harness: codex,gemini` or `# skip: opencode`) so specific rules only sync to designated harnesses. Currently it's all-or-nothing. Users with harness-specific rules (e.g., a Gemini-only Google Cloud rule) need fine-grained control over what goes where.

items.title: Pre-Sync Secret Scrubber
items.description: Scan source configs for secrets (API keys, tokens, connection strings) before syncing to targets — especially important when syncing to team-shared or remote targets. Warn and block sync if secrets are detected. Prevents accidental credential leakage when config files contain inline auth values.

items.title: Natural Language Rule Authoring
items.description: A /sync-new-rule command that takes a plain English description ('always use conventional commits', 'never use console.log in production code') and generates properly formatted rules for CLAUDE.md AND equivalent formats for all target harnesses simultaneously. Eliminates format-learning overhead when adding new rules.

items.title: Sync Dry Run with Impact Scoring
items.description: Enhance the existing sandbox command with an impact score — estimating how much behavior change each target harness would experience from a pending sync. E.g., '14 rule changes: HIGH impact on Codex, LOW impact on Gemini (only 2 rules changed).' Helps users triage what needs attention before committing.

items.title: Multi-Repo Config Federation
items.description: A monorepo-aware mode that federates configs: shared rules live at repo root and sync everywhere, while sub-package rules are scoped. Also supports pulling rules FROM a central org-level repo into local project configs. Solves the org-wide standards problem — one team maintains the base ruleset, everyone inherits it.

items.title: Harness-Specific Skill Variants
items.description: Allow a skill to have a 'base' version plus harness-specific override variants. E.g., the /commit skill behaves slightly differently in Gemini CLI due to its different tool model — define the variant once, and HarnessSync deploys the right version to each harness. Currently users must maintain entirely separate skill files.

items.title: Config Size Optimizer
items.description: Analyze CLAUDE.md and rule files for redundancy — duplicate rules, contradictory instructions, dead rules that reference non-existent skills. Suggest a trimmed config that is shorter (faster token loading) while preserving intent. Addresses the accumulation problem where CLAUDE.md grows unbounded over months.

items.title: Branch-Aware Sync
items.description: Detect git branch switches and re-sync if the new branch has different .claude/ configs (e.g., a feature branch with experimental rules). Ensures the harness always reflects the current branch's intended AI behavior, not the main branch config. Especially useful for teams that version AI configs alongside code.

items.title: VS Code / IDE Extension Bridge
items.description: A VS Code extension that surfaces HarnessSync status in the status bar and allows triggering syncs without leaving the editor. Shows a colored indicator (green=synced, yellow=drift detected, red=error) and opens the diff viewer inline. Reduces context switching for users who live in VS Code but use CLI harnesses.

items.title: Config Dependency Graph
items.description: Visualize which skills invoke other skills, which rules reference which tools, and how MCP servers are used across skills. Output as ASCII in terminal or as a Mermaid diagram. Helps users understand the 'blast radius' of changing a rule or removing a skill before they do it.

items.title: Harness Health Score
items.description: A composite health score (0-100) for each synced harness showing: config freshness, successful recent syncs, capability coverage percentage, and known errors. Replace the binary 'synced/not synced' status with a nuanced health signal that surfaces what actually matters.

items.title: Exportable Sync Report for Teams
items.description: Generate a shareable HTML or Markdown report showing full sync state across all harnesses — suitable for a team wiki or PR description. Includes capability gaps, unsynced items, and last-sync timestamps. Solves the 'how do I communicate our AI tooling setup to a new team member?' problem.

items.title: Conditional Rules Engine
items.description: Support conditional rules in CLAUDE.md that only activate based on project context (detected language, framework, file patterns). HarnessSync translates these conditions to each harness's native format where supported, or compiles them into unconditional rules with comments. Makes a single config file serve multiple project types.

items.title: Sync Event Webhooks
items.description: Fire webhooks on sync events (sync-complete, drift-detected, sync-failed) to integrate with Slack, Discord, PagerDuty, or custom endpoints. Teams using shared configs can get Slack notifications when a teammate syncs a breaking rule change. Bridges HarnessSync into existing team notification workflows.

items.title: Config Time Travel
items.description: Given a git commit SHA or date, reconstruct what the synced config looked like at that point in time. Useful for debugging: 'the Codex session from last Tuesday behaved differently — what was the config then?' Works by replaying sync snapshots against the git history of CLAUDE.md.

items.title: Harness A/B Config Testing
items.description: Define two rule variants and automatically split them across harnesses for comparison. E.g., variant A goes to Codex, variant B goes to Gemini — run the same task on both and compare outputs. Helps users empirically determine which rule phrasing produces better AI behavior across harnesses.

items.title: Minimal Config Extractor
items.description: Given a specific task type ('I only use this harness for code review'), extract the minimal config subset that matters for that use case and sync only that. Reduces noise in target harness configs and speeds up context loading. Solves the 'my AGENTS.md has 200 lines but I only use Codex for diffs' problem.

items.title: Auto-Detect New Harnesses
items.description: Watch PATH and common install directories for new AI coding tool installations and automatically prompt to add them to the sync matrix. As new harnesses emerge (e.g., a new open-source tool gains traction), users don't have to manually run /sync-add-harness — HarnessSync finds them.

items.title: Rule Effectiveness Tagging
items.description: Let users tag rules with effectiveness metadata ('this rule reduced hallucinations', 'this rule caused too many refusals', 'needs refinement') and track that metadata through syncs. Over time, builds a personal knowledge base of which rules work. Helps teams curate high-signal configs and retire dead rules.

items.title: Harness Migration Assistant
items.description: Interactive wizard for users migrating FROM another harness (Cursor, Aider) TO Claude Code as their primary. Reads the source harness config, imports rules/conventions into CLAUDE.md format, then syncs back out to all other harnesses. Lowers the adoption barrier for new Claude Code users who already have mature configs elsewhere.

items.title: Staging Environment for Config Changes
items.description: Before syncing a config change to ALL harnesses, sync it first to a single 'staging' harness for a user-defined period. If no issues arise, promote to all targets. Prevents a bad rule change from degrading every harness simultaneously — gives users a blast-radius control mechanism for risky config experiments.

items.title: MCP Tool Stub Generation for Non-MCP Harnesses
items.description: When syncing to harnesses without MCP support, auto-generate shell script stubs that approximate MCP tool behavior (e.g., a bash script that wraps the same underlying API). Users who rely on MCP tools in Claude Code get at least degraded-but-functional equivalents elsewhere.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.

items.title: MCP Tool Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Show which MCP tools work in which harnesses and flag tools that will silently fail. Users waste hours debugging why an MCP capability works in Claude Code but not in Gemini.
